insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for diabetes is 12.2%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 8.8 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, diabetes is most associated with area deprivation index rank (Spearman rho=0.692, n=32,193).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.
Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 20.2 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (17.4%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (17.4%).
